model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, doubling batch size increases unique training examples seen per run and usually improves early-loss stability for this compact CNN on CIFAR-10 without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size increased to 128 under a fixed-step budget, a higher learning rate is a strong next move to speed optimization progress within the same 256 updates.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The previous step regressed validation loss, so disabling the scheduler is the safest high-confidence recovery to restore the stronger constant-LR behavior at 1e-3 over 256 fixed steps.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Switching to AdamW decouples weight decay from adaptive moments and often yields better validation loss than Adam at the same learning rate in fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps, slightly reducing weight decay can improve fit speed while keeping AdamW regularization benefits, which is often favorable for early-budget validation loss.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With only 256 steps, lowering AdamW beta2 can improve responsiveness of second-moment estimates and often yields faster early optimization on CIFAR-10.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The beta2 reduction hurt validation loss, so reverting to 0.999 restores the better-performing optimizer dynamics.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"A modest width increase should improve representational capacity and can lower validation loss within 256 steps while keeping the model compact and the edit simple.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After widening convolution channels, increasing classifier hidden width is a small aligned capacity bump that can improve utilization of richer features within the same training budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The hidden-layer expansion caused a clear regression, so reverting restores the stronger compact head configuration.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing model width, a slightly lower learning rate can improve stability and final validation loss within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The lower learning rate regressed validation loss, so reverting to 1e-3 restores the best-performing optimization setting.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step mode, increasing batch size to 256 exposes more unique samples within 256 updates and can improve validation loss through better gradient estimates.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After doubling batch size to 256, a modest learning-rate increase is a high-value next test to improve optimization progress under the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The recent LR increase helped, so a further moderate step up is a strong next probe for better fixed-budget optimization at batch size 256.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Given consistent gains from higher learning rate at batch size 256, another controlled increase is the most promising remaining move for additional fixed-step improvement.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Increasing to 1.6e-3 regressed, so reverting to 1.4e-3 restores the best-known learning-rate setting.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With stronger optimization from larger batch and LR, slightly reducing AdamW weight decay may improve fit within the fixed-step budget while retaining regularization.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The weight-decay reduction regressed validation loss, so reverting to 5e-5 restores the stronger regularization setting.","secondary_modes":[]}
